rulings.Case Title: LONNA WAIS VS. ROBERT L FARMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Duces Tecum Seeking The Records Of William Shannahan And Req For Fees, Costs And Sanctions; Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF LONNA WAIS' Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Duces Tecum Seeking The Records Of William Shannahan And Req For Fees, Costs And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 1000 PENNSYLVANIA AVENUE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A VS. QUINLAN CONSTRUCTION, A CALIFORNIA SOLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Oppose Sunset Stucco'S Appl For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANTS SEAN CARLIN, WILLIAM LORDAN, CHRISTINA LORDAN'S MOTION To Oppose Sunset Stucco'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. The settlement is determined to be in good faith. C&L did not sustain its burden to shown that the settlement is outside the Tech-Bilt ballpark. C&L objection which relies solely on plaintiff's claimed damages rather than evaluating those damages is insufficient to defeat the application for good faith. (Horton v. Superior Court (1987) 194 Cal. App. 3d 727, 735-36). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TUCKER VS. LORRIE'S TRAVEL & TOURS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT RUFINO MEJIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. Plaintiff and Defendant/Cross-Complainant Lorrie's presented evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Mejia was driving the van that was involved in the accident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TUCKER VS. LORRIE'S TRAVEL & TOURS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Tucker'S Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 4. Plaintiff Joseph Tucker'S Motion For Sanctions. Denied. Apart from the fact that Code of Civil Procedure 128.5 is not applicable to any case filed in the last sixteen years, plaintiff has not shown that Mr. Mejia's motion is frivolous, filed in bad faith or with any intention to mislead the court. Moreover, since his deposition had not completed, there is no basis to assert that Mr. Mejia's declaration conflicted with his deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Discovery And To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT ROXUL, LLC. AND LUXOR CABS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Judge Pro Tem discloses that he is employed by the law firm of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson. Carlson, Calladine & Peterson utilizes the services of the accounting department of Defendants' counsel, Hassard Bonnington for certain accounts payable and accounts receivable services. The Judge Pro Tem does not have the power of selection over the vendors used by Carlson, Calladine & Peterson for its accounting services, does not negotiate the contracts for such services, and does not have a direct financial interest in such services. For these reasons, the Judge Pro Tem does not believe recusal is warranted. As to the merits of the motions, no opposition was filed. The motions are granted. The facts listed in the requests for admission are deemed admitted. Plaintiff shall respond to the Form Interrogatories within 20 days without objection. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $477.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bkaplan@ccplaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ARMENTA VS. STEFAN JAMES DALKERT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 16. DEFENDANT AARON BLACKLEDGE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to allege that the defendant knew, or should have known, that the driver was incompetent or unfit to drive the vehicle, and is now given leave to do so, if it can do so in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NIETO FINE ART SAN FRANCISCO, LLC, A CALIFORNIA et al VS. JOIE DE VIVRE HOSPITALITY, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 19, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT BARBARY INSURANCE BROKERAGE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to third cause of action. The allegations supporting the claim for breach of fiduciary duty are indistinguishable from those supporting the claim for professional negligence. Given the latter, there is no need for the former. Moreover, no California authority supports a breach of fiduciary claim under these circumstances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
